div.head: The Secretary of State to the Ambassador in China (Stuart)
div.p:
1242. Please deliver personally following message to Foreign Minister from me in reply to his message forwarded in urtel 1576 August 25:
“Thank you for your message forwarded to me by Ambassador Stuart. I have just received information regarding the emergency financial and economic measures proclaimed by President Chiang and am now having a careful study made of them. I am gratified at your
determination, as well as that of your colleagues, to enforce measures designed to help meet China’s serious economic problems and view sympathetically measures of self-help taken by the Chinese Government.
I look forward to seeing you at the UN Assembly in Paris and to having the opportunity of discussions on these and several other matters. My warm regards to you.”
